title: South Carolina Association of Disability Examiners (SCADE) — Constitution & By-Laws
description: Constitution and by-laws of the South Carolina Association of Disability Examiners (SCADE), a division of NADE in the Southeast Region. The constitution covers name, definitions, purpose, membership, governance by an Executive Committee (officers plus office representatives from the three regional offices, administrative office, and Lexington state-claims office), meetings, and amendments; the by-laws cover membership/dues (January 1 fiscal year), Executive Committee duties and officers, terms of office, nominations and elections, committees, standard operating procedures (including conference-attendee funding and the Barry Cooper Leadership Award), and email/conference-call voting. Appendices include the Conference Attendee Selection/Funding Procedures and the Barry Cooper Leadership Award nomination and selection guidelines with a sample nomination.

source.publisher: National Association of Disability Examiners (NADE) — South Carolina (SCADE/SCADES) Chapter
source.title: South Carolina Association of Disability Examiners Constitution & By-Laws

notes: Authoritative: this is the markitdown-normalized markdown conversion of the sibling source document South-Carolina-Const-Bylaws.doc. The conversion is clean and includes lengthy appendices (Conference Attendee funding procedures, the Barry Cooper Leadership Award guidelines, and a sample award nomination, which duplicates a block of award text). The document uses both 'SCADE' and 'SCADES' (the latter expands as South Carolina Association of Disability Evaluation Specialists in the award sections). No overall adoption/revision date is stated, so temporal_coverage is omitted (the sample award text references fiscal years 2005-2006 illustratively, not as the document date). Related to the NADE regional constitutions/bylaws link index.
